title: Planning Extraction Candidates
goal: Implement #465 by defining which planning-adjacent capabilities should stay internal, remain optional extensions, or become future module extraction candidates.
non_goals: Do not create new packages in this slice.
Do not treat the capability map as a backlog.

intent.outcome: Extraction candidates have clear boundaries, reasons, evidence requirements, and blockers.
intent.constraints: Keep planning core narrow and avoid adding first-contact surfaces.
intent.latitude: Use docs/agent-os-capabilities.md, payload classification, and current optional payload boundaries to shape the candidate map.
intent.escalation: Escalate if a candidate requires immediate package creation or broad architecture work outside #465.

execution.next_step: Inspect docs/agent-os-capabilities.md, payload-surface-classification.json, and #465 acceptance criteria to draft the extraction-candidate map.
execution.proof: planning package tests if package surfaces change, workspace CLI tests if root query surfaces change, ruff, proof selector, and planning doctor

invariants: No new package is created in this slice.
Follow-up issues are created only for stable candidate shapes.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #461: make Agentic Planning smaller by default and clearer by boundary.

required_continuation.required follow-on detail: #466 still needs capability-map follow-through after extraction candidates land.
required_continuation.activation trigger: after #465 closeout, promote #466 or close #461 if validation proves the parent satisfied

parent_lane.title: Reclassify planning payload into core, optional extensions, and extraction candidates

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Define extraction candidates for review, intake, reconciliation, and advanced agent-operation capabilities.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Do not create new packages or grow default startup/install surfaces.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact format and location of the candidate map if it avoids becoming another ordinary startup surface.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The solution requires module extraction implementation instead of boundary definition.

context_budget.live working set: state, this execplan, #461/#465, docs/agent-os-capabilities.md, payload classification, optional-extension archive
context_budget.externalize before shift: record extraction candidates, criteria, blockers, and any stable follow-up issues
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Next active issue is #465 planning extraction candidates.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Before creating any new package or broad root-level surface.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Make the package streamlined, lightweight, frictionless, cheap, modular, and small while preserving leverage.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Separate capability boundaries before adding or extracting implementation.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can planning stay small by naming adjacent capabilities and extraction criteria rather than absorbing them?

active_milestone.scope: Keep this execution thread bounded to extraction-candidate definition and follow-up routing.

immediate_next_action: Add a compact candidate map with boundaries, extraction criteria, blockers, and stable follow-up issues if justified.
invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.
Keep planning core narrow and avoid making candidate analysis part of ordinary startup.
validation_commands: uv run ruff check src tests packages/planning/src packages/planning/tests
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: Extraction candidates have clear boundaries and reasons.
Each candidate has evidence required before extraction.
Planning core remains narrow and does not absorb every agent-OS capability.
Follow-up issues are only created when a candidate has stable enough shape.

closure_check.evidence carried forward: #465 owns extraction-candidate boundaries after #464 optional enablement.
closure_check.reopen trigger: planning-adjacent capability boundaries stay ambiguous enough that planning keeps absorbing non-core surfaces

drift_log: 2026-04-27: Promoted after #464 added explicit optional enablement.
